topic.ko: 인생 이야기 · 일 · 가족 · 배움 · 역사적 인물

core_title.fr: Des personnes qui ont changé leur vie

core_reading_html.en: Many people worked hard in the past. Mattie started work when she was very young. She loved to learn and later could read and write. Her family was very important to her. Other famous leaders and thinkers also changed history. They showed great courage, shared new ideas, and left an important legacy. Their stories still give us hope today.
core_reading_html.ja: 昔、多くの 人々 は一生懸命働きました。マティは幼いころに 仕事 を始めました。彼女は 学ぶ ことが好きで、後に 読む ことや 書く ことができるようになりました。家族 は彼女にとって大切でした。ほかの有名な 指導者 たちも歴史を変えました。彼らは大きな 勇気 を示し、新しい 考え を広め、重要な 遺産 を残しました。その物語は今も私たちに 希望 を与えています。
core_reading_html.fr: Dans le passé, beaucoup de personnes ont travaillé dur. Mattie a commencé le travail très jeune. Elle aimait apprendre et a ensuite pu lire et écrire. Sa famille était très importante. D'autres grands dirigeants ont changé l'histoire. Ils ont montré du courage, partagé des idées nouvelles et laissé un grand héritage. Leurs histoires donnent encore de l'espoir aujourd'hui.
core_reading_html.ko: 과거에는 많은 사람들 이 열심히 살았습니다. 마티는 아주 어린 나이에 일 을 시작했습니다. 그녀는 배움 을 좋아했고, 나중에는 읽기 와 쓰기 를 할 수 있게 되었습니다. 가족 은 그녀에게 매우 중요했습니다. 다른 유명한 지도자 들도 역사를 바꾸었습니다. 그들은 큰 용기 를 보여 주었고 새로운 생각 을 나누었으며 중요한 유산 을 남겼습니다. 그들의 이야기는 오늘날에도 우리에게 희망 을 줍니다.

en.definition: to look at words and understand them
something important left for the future

fr.definition: ce qui reste pour le futur

script.speaker: A
script.text: What time does Mattie start her day?

script.speaker: B
script.text: First she has a bath, then she cleans the house.

script.speaker: A
script.text: What does she do after that?

script.speaker: B
script.text: She sits outside on her verandah and thinks about her past life.

script.speaker: B
script.text: Then she writes poems about it.

script.speaker: B
script.text: When she was eight years old.

script.speaker: A
script.text: How many hours did she work?

script.speaker: B
script.text: She married Hubert when she was sixteen.

script.speaker: A
script.text: How many children did she have?

script.speaker: B
script.text: Six children. Five sons, then a daughter, Lily.

script.speaker: B
script.text: Sixty-five years ago, just before Lily was born.

en.title: Learning to read and write
en.label: Listening 1: Mattie Smith in her own words

script.speaker: Mattie
script.text: I worked from 6.00 in the morning until 10.00 at night. Sixteen hours in the cotton fields and I only earned $2 a day.

script.speaker: Mattie
script.text: I sure hated that job but I loved the poems in my head. I really wanted to learn to read and write.

script.speaker: Mattie
script.text: When I was sixteen I married Hubert. Hubert died just before my daughter Lily was born.

script.speaker: Mattie
script.text: I looked after my family alone. My children all learned to read and write – that was important to me.

script.speaker: Mattie
script.text: I didn't learn to read and write until I was 86, and now I have three books of poems.

script.speaker: A
script.text: À quelle heure Mattie commence-t-elle sa journée ?

script.speaker: B
script.text: D'abord elle prend un bain, ensuite elle fait le ménage.

script.speaker: B
script.text: Elle s'assoit dehors sur sa véranda et elle pense à sa vie passée.

script.speaker: B
script.text: Ensuite elle écrit des poèmes sur sa vie.

script.speaker: A
script.text: À quel âge a-t-elle commencé à travailler ?

script.speaker: B
script.text: Elle a épousé Hubert à seize ans.

script.speaker: B
script.text: Six enfants. Cinq garçons, puis une fille, Lily.

script.speaker: B
script.text: Il y a soixante-cinq ans, juste avant la naissance de Lily.

ja.title: Apprendre à lire et à écrire
ja.label: Compréhension orale 1 : Les propres mots de Mattie Smith

script.speaker: Mattie
script.text: J'ai travaillé de 6h du matin à 10h du soir. Seize heures dans les champs de coton et je ne gagnais que 2 dollars par jour.

script.speaker: Mattie
script.text: Je détestais vraiment ce travail, mais j'adorais les poèmes dans ma tête. Je voulais tellement apprendre à lire et à écrire.

script.speaker: Mattie
script.text: À seize ans, j'ai épousé Hubert. Hubert est mort juste avant la naissance de ma fille Lily.

script.speaker: Mattie
script.text: J'ai élevé ma famille toute seule. Tous mes enfants ont appris à lire et à écrire – c'était important pour moi.

script.speaker: Mattie
script.text: Je n'ai appris à lire et à écrire qu'à 86 ans, et maintenant j'ai trois recueils de poésie.

script.speaker: A
script.text: À quelle heure Mattie commence-t-elle sa journée ?

script.speaker: B
script.text: D'abord elle prend un bain, ensuite elle fait le ménage.

script.speaker: B
script.text: Elle s'assoit dehors sur sa véranda et elle pense à sa vie passée.

script.speaker: B
script.text: Ensuite elle écrit des poèmes sur sa vie.

script.speaker: A
script.text: À quel âge a-t-elle commencé à travailler ?

script.speaker: B
script.text: Elle a épousé Hubert à seize ans.

script.speaker: B
script.text: Six enfants. Cinq garçons, puis une fille, Lily.

script.speaker: B
script.text: Il y a soixante-cinq ans, juste avant la naissance de Lily.

fr.title: Apprendre à lire et à écrire
fr.label: Compréhension orale 1 : Les propres mots de Mattie Smith

script.speaker: Mattie
script.text: J'ai travaillé de 6h du matin à 10h du soir. Seize heures dans les champs de coton et je ne gagnais que 2 dollars par jour.

script.speaker: Mattie
script.text: Je détestais vraiment ce travail, mais j'adorais les poèmes dans ma tête. Je voulais tellement apprendre à lire et à écrire.

script.speaker: Mattie
script.text: À seize ans, j'ai épousé Hubert. Hubert est mort juste avant la naissance de ma fille Lily.

script.speaker: Mattie
script.text: J'ai élevé ma famille toute seule. Tous mes enfants ont appris à lire et à écrire – c'était important pour moi.

script.speaker: Mattie
script.text: Je n'ai appris à lire et à écrire qu'à 86 ans, et maintenant j'ai trois recueils de poésie.

script.speaker: A
script.text: 마티는 아침에 몇 시에 하루를 시작해?

script.speaker: B
script.text: 먼저 목욕을 하고, 그다음에 집을 청소해.

script.speaker: B
script.text: 베란다에 나가서 앉아 과거의 삶에 대해 생각한대.

script.speaker: B
script.text: 여섯 명. 아들 다섯, 그리고 딸 릴리가 있었어.

script.speaker: 마티
script.text: 저는 아침 6시부터 밤 10시까지 일했어요. 면화밭에서 16시간 동안 일하고 겨우 하루에 2달러를 벌었죠.

script.speaker: 마티
script.text: 그 일은 정말 싫었지만, 머릿속에 떠오르는 시들은 사랑했어요. 읽고 쓰는 법을 꼭 배우고 싶었죠.

script.speaker: 마티
script.text: 열여섯 살에 휴버트와 결혼했어요. 휴버트는 딸 릴리가 태어나기 직전에 돌아가셨어요.

script.speaker: 마티
script.text: 그 후로 혼자서 가족을 부양했어요. 제 아이들은 모두 읽고 쓰기를 배웠죠. 그게 저에게는 정말 중요했어요.

script.speaker: 마티
script.text: 제가 읽고 쓰기를 배운 건 86살이 되어서였어요. 지금은 시집 세 권을 냈답니다.

extra_reading_title.ja: 歴史上の人物：リーダーシップと人生

extra_reading_html.en:
George Washington (1732–1799) was the first President of the United States. He became President in 1789. He was born in Virginia. His family owned a big farm and had slaves. He didn't have much education. He was a farmer, a soldier, and a politician. He loved the life of a farmer. In 1759 he married a widow called Martha Custis. They didn't have any children. He fought the British in the War of Independence. He started the building of the White House, but he never lived in it. He died on his farm in 1799.

Margaret Thatcher (1925–2013) was the first woman prime minister in Europe. She became Prime Minister of Britain in 1979. She was born above a shop in Grantham. Her father was a grocer. She studied chemistry at Oxford University. In 1951 she married Denis Thatcher, a rich businessman. They had twins. She loved politics and didn't need much sleep – only four hours a night. She was called 'The Iron Lady'. She was Prime Minister for eleven years. She resigned in 1990, and she was in tears when she left.
extra_reading_html.ja:
徳川家康 (1543–1616)は、江戸幕府の初代将軍です。彼は三河国で生まれ、長い戦乱の時代を終わらせ、平和な時代を築きました。鷹狩りを好み、健康には非常に気を使っていました。彼には多くの子供がいました。彼が築いた江戸（現在の東京）は、その後世界最大の都市の一つとなりました。1616年に駿府で亡くなりました。

与謝野晶子 (1878–1942)は、日本の有名な詩人です。大阪で生まれ、情熱的な詩集『みだれ髪』で知られています。彼女は女性の権利を強く主張し、多くの評論やエッセイを書きました。彼女には12人の子供がいました。生涯を通じて新しい文学の形を追求し、日本の近代文学に大きな影響を与えました。
extra_reading_html.fr:
Napoléon Bonaparte (1769–1821) était un général français et le premier Empereur des Français. Né en Corse, il a conquis une grande partie de l'Europe grâce à ses stratégies militaires. Il a créé le Code civil en 1804. Il était connu pour son ambition et son intelligence tactique. Napoléon a été exilé à Sainte-Hélène à la fin de sa vie, où il est mort en 1821.

Marie Curie (1867–1934) était une physicienne et chimiste polonaise naturalisée française. Elle est célèbre pour ses travaux sur la radioactivité. Elle a été la première femme à recevoir un prix Nobel. Elle a étudié à Paris, où elle a rencontré son mari, Pierre Curie. Ensemble, ils ont découvert le polonium et le radium. Elle est morte en 1934, laissant un héritage scientifique immense.
extra_reading_html.ko:
세종대왕 (1397–1450)은 조선의 제4대 왕입니다. 그는 백성들을 사랑하여 1443년에 우리글인 훈민정음을 창제했습니다. 과학과 예술을 발전시켰으며, 많은 인재를 등용했습니다. 그는 몸이 약했지만, 밤늦게까지 공부하기를 좋아했습니다. 오늘날 그는 한국인들이 가장 존경하는 위대한 지도자 중 한 명으로 기억됩니다.

유관순 (1902–1920)은 한국의 독립운동가입니다. 1919년 3.1 운동 때 만세 시위를 주도했습니다. 그녀는 어린 나이에도 불구하고 나라를 위해 끝까지 용기 있게 싸웠습니다. 감옥에 갇혔지만, 그녀의 애국심은 꺾이지 않았습니다. 그녀는 조국의 자유를 위해 18세의 나이로 순국했습니다.

en.question: George Washington was the first President of the United States.
George Washington lived in the White House for many years.
en.explanation: False. He started the building of it, but never lived in it.

en.question: Margaret Thatcher studied chemistry at Oxford.
en.explanation: True. She studied chemistry at Oxford University.

en.question: Margaret Thatcher was Prime Minister for 20 years.
en.explanation: False. She was Prime Minister for eleven years.

en.question: Margaret Thatcher required a lot of sleep.
en.explanation: False. She only needed four hours of sleep a night.

ja.question: 徳川家康は江戸幕府の初代将軍でした。
ja.explanation: 正解です。彼は江戸幕府を開きました。

ja.question: 家康は戦乱の時代を終わらせることはできませんでした。
ja.explanation: 間違いです。彼は長い戦乱の時代を終わらせました。

ja.question: 与謝野晶子は詩人としてだけでなく評論も書きました。
ja.explanation: 正解です。彼女は多くの評論やエッセイも執筆しました。

ja.question: 晶子には子供がいませんでした。
ja.explanation: 間違いです。彼女には12人の子供がいました。

ja.question: 家康は1616年に亡くなりました。
ja.explanation: 正解です。彼は1616年に駿府で亡くなりました。

fr.question: Napoléon est né en Corse.
fr.explanation: Vrai. Il est né en Corse.

fr.question: Napoléon a créé le Code civil en 1904.
Marie Curie était la première femme à recevoir un prix Nobel.
Marie Curie a découvert le radium toute seule.
fr.explanation: Faux. Elle l'a découvert avec son mari, Pierre Curie.

fr.question: Napoléon est mort en exil à Sainte-Hélène.
fr.explanation: Vrai. Il y est décédé en 1821.

ko.question: 세종대왕은 과학 발전에 관심이 없었습니다.
ko.explanation: 틀렸습니다. 그는 과학과 예술을 크게 발전시켰습니다.

ko.question: 유관순은 3.1 운동 때 만세 시위를 주도했습니다.
세종대왕은 건강이 아주 좋았습니다.
ko.explanation: 틀렸습니다. 지문에 그는 몸이 약했다고 적혀 있습니다.

en.text: When did you last have a holiday? Last August. Where did you go? To Spain.
Yes, I did. / No, I didn't.
Did you pass the test? Yes, I did. / No, I didn't.

grammar_html.en:
1. Past Simple – Regular Verbs

For regular verbs, we add -ed to form the past simple.

  • • start → started / work → worked / create → created
  • • live → lived (add only -d because the verb ends in -e)

2. Past Simple – Negative and Questions (did)

We use did not (didn't) + base form for negatives. We use did + subject + base form for questions.

  • • Negative: She didn't learn to read until she was 86.
  • • Question: When did she learn to read?

3. Past Simple – Irregular Verbs

Irregular verbs have special past forms that are not made with -ed.

  • • go → went / have → had / become → became / buy → bought / meet → met
  • • be → was/were / can → could

content_check_question.en: The text explains the Past Simple tense using regular and irregular verbs, and includes biographies of Mattie Smith, George Washington, and Margaret Thatcher. How do you form the Past Simple of regular verbs? How do you make negative sentences and questions in the Past Simple? What are some common irregular verbs?

content_check_keywords.en: Past Simple, regular verbs, irregular verbs, negative, questions, did, silent letters, special occasions

content_check_placeholder.en: Explain how to form the Past Simple for regular verbs, how to use 'didn't' for negatives, and give examples of irregular verbs...

deepening_question.en: The unit includes a conversation about special occasions (birthday, New Year's Eve, Thanksgiving, etc.). What special occasions are important in your country? How do people celebrate them? What do you say to someone on these days in your language?
